title: Require & verify TDD RED-run evidence from the backend-impl agent

steps.description: RED-first: write tests/unit/test_tdd_red_evidence_contract.py (fails); then add the mandatory-RED workflow step + tdd_red_evidence result-contract field to agents/claude/backend-impl.md and agents/opencode/backend-impl.md; mirror the contract into templates/design/{Implementation,SelfAssess,CodeReview}_Prompt_Template.md and the ai-dev/templates/ copies; run `iw sync-agents`; tick item 0.4 DONE in ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md. Record tdd_red_evidence for the new guard test in the result JSON.

Review S01: agent/template edits consistent across all 8 files; guard test asserts the right marker strings; result-JSON example includes tdd_red_evidence; `iw sync-agents` was run (.claude/agents/backend-impl.md == agents/claude/backend-impl.md, .opencode/agents/backend-impl.md == agents/opencode/backend-impl.md); item 0.4 ticked; no out-of-scope edits.

Global review: the agent <-> template <-> guard-test chain is internally consistent and the new contract is unambiguous; the mandatory-vs-optional split in the CodeReview template (mandatory: confirm presence + reason about RED-against-pre-change-code; optional: stash-recheck) is stated clearly; no scope creep.
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
